Scope filtering at query time (not cache time) for per-session results

decisions.issue: No authentication on chat endpoint - any tool call is executed if tool exists in registry, regardless of user intent or scope configuration.
decisions.recommendation: Implement session-scoped authorization via Redis scope snapshots. Every tool call must pass scope validation before execution.
decisions.mitigation_plan: Phase 1: Add scope validation in tool executor (agent_loop). Store allowed scopes in Redis per session. Reject calls to tools outside allowed scopes with clear error message.

decisions.issue: Scope enforcement is frontend-only - scopes filter what AI sees in page context but backend executes any valid tool call regardless of configured scopes.
decisions.recommendation: Backend must enforce scopes independently. Defense-in-depth: frontend filters context, backend validates execution.
decisions.mitigation_plan: Add server-side scope check in agent_loop._call_tool() before executing handler. Compare tool scope against session allowed scopes from Redis.

decisions.issue: Permission config (3-layer merge) is not bound to sessions - config changes affect all sessions immediately, including in-flight agent loops.
decisions.recommendation: Snapshot permissions at session creation. In-flight sessions use their snapshot, not live config.
decisions.mitigation_plan: Store merged permission config in Redis alongside scope snapshot at session creation. Agent loop reads from snapshot, not live config.

decisions.issue: Prompt injection could trick LLM into calling tools outside intended scope. Even with backend enforcement, crafted prompts could exploit allowed tools maliciously.
decisions.recommendation: Layer defenses: scope enforcement prevents cross-scope calls, input sanitization reduces injection surface, tool result validation prevents data exfiltration.
decisions.mitigation_plan: 1. Backend scope enforcement (primary defense). 2. Rate limiting on tool calls per session. 3. Audit logging of all tool calls. 4. Consider tool result size limits.

decisions.issue: Scope change race condition in agent loop - if user toggles scope while agent is mid-execution, tool calls may execute against stale scope set.
decisions.recommendation: Check scope validity at each iteration boundary. If scope changed mid-loop, complete current iteration but block new tool calls outside new scope.
decisions.mitigation_plan: Agent loop checks Redis scope snapshot version at each iteration start. If version changed, reload scopes. Current iteration tools already dispatched are allowed to complete.

decisions.issue: WebSocket chat events may leak data across sessions if session isolation is not properly enforced in the streaming endpoint.
decisions.recommendation: Ensure WebSocket connections are bound to specific sessions. Validate session ownership on connection and each event.
decisions.mitigation_plan: WebSocket handler validates session_id ownership on connect. Events include session_id, client-side filters by active session.

decisions.issue: Redis memory growth from scope snapshots - each session creates a Redis key for scope data, potentially accumulating if sessions are not properly cleaned up.
decisions.recommendation: Use TTL-aligned keys. Session scope key expires with session TTL (1h default). No manual cleanup needed.
decisions.mitigation_plan: Set EXPIRE on scope snapshot keys equal to session TTL. Monitor Redis memory usage.

decisions.issue: getToolContract could be used for reconnaissance - AI or malicious prompt could enumerate all tools to discover system capabilities beyond intended scope.
decisions.recommendation: getToolContract must respect session scopes - only return contracts for tools within allowed scopes.
decisions.mitigation_plan: listAvailableTools and getToolContract both filter by session scopes. Requesting contract for out-of-scope tool returns permission denied, not tool details.

decisions.issue: Context staleness - page context in system prompt reflects state at message send time, but tool calls may change state during execution (e.g., task status changes).
decisions.recommendation: Accept staleness for page context (refreshed on next message). Tool results always return current state. Document this as expected behavior.
decisions.mitigation_plan: Page context is re-serialized on each user message. Tool results reflect live state. No mid-conversation context refresh needed.

decisions.issue: Token budget exceeded if app map + page context + tool contracts + conversation history approach model context limit.
decisions.recommendation: Budget: app map ~500, page context ~800, max 3 tool contracts ~600 = ~1900 tokens for AI context. Well within limits. Monitor and add hard cap if needed.
decisions.mitigation_plan: Set maxChars on serializer (already 4000). App map endpoint respects maxChars. Log context token counts for monitoring.

decisions.issue: Over-engineering risk - O-024 has 6 KRs spanning auth, context, tools, scopes, UX. Full implementation could take weeks and delay other objectives.
decisions.recommendation: Phase deliveries: Phase 1 (backend scope enforcement + fix tool execution), Phase 2 (progressive context + getToolContract), Phase 3 (app map + polish).
decisions.mitigation_plan: Break into 3 phases. Phase 1 delivers security (KR-1, KR-2) and fixes core tool execution. Phase 2 adds intelligence (KR-3, KR-4). Phase 3 adds completeness (KR-5, KR-6).

decisions.issue: Error message information leakage - detailed error messages from tool execution could expose internal system details (file paths, Redis keys, stack traces).
decisions.recommendation: Sanitize error messages returned to LLM. Log full details server-side, return generic errors to AI.
decisions.mitigation_plan: Tool executor catches exceptions, logs full error, returns sanitized message to LLM (e.g., 'Tool execution failed: permission denied' not full traceback).

decisions.issue: WS chat.token lacks block_type discriminator for structured stream view
decisions.recommendation: Add block_type field (values: token, thinking) to chat.token WS payload in llm_chat.py on_event callback
decisions.reasoning: Both StreamEvent(token) and StreamEvent(thinking) mapped to same chat.token WS event with only session_id+content. Debug tab needs to distinguish thinking from response text. Minimal backward-compatible change.

decisions.issue: Empty scopes [] falsy-converted to None, disabling ALL scope enforcement — LLM sessions with explicitly empty scopes got unrestricted tool access
decisions.recommendation: Use direct value or is-not-None checks instead of falsy conversion for scope lists
decisions.reasoning: Python falsy check `scopes if scopes else None` converts [] to None. In AgentLoop, None means no enforcement. Fixed 7 instances across llm_chat.py (3) and tool_registry.py (4).

decisions.issue: Missing guideline detail route in useScopeResolver — /projects/[slug]/guidelines/[id] fell through to default dashboard scope
decisions.recommendation: Added regex pattern for guideline detail route before the list route in ROUTE_RULES
decisions.reasoning: AI sidebar showed wrong context on guideline detail pages because the route had no matching rule.
decisions.resolution_notes: Added { pattern: /^/projects/([^/]+)/guidelines/([^/]+)$/ } to ROUTE_RULES
